title: Populate email field based on assigned user
excerpt:
Dear all, 
how can I populate email field based on assigned user? Since the Monday knows the email for each user, how I can populate the email field once I assign the user? 
Thanks in advance :heart:

posts.author: DaliborVasiljevic (Dalibor)
posts.cookedHtml:
Dear all,

how can I populate email field based on assigned user? Since the Monday knows the email for each user, how I can populate the email field once I assign the user?

Thanks in advance

posts.author: malia.monday (Malia)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi there, these users being assigned to an item, and you want the email address associated with their user to populate into another column? Is that correct?

If so, currently there is not an automation that would populate this result into an Email Column. That said, you could use a Formula Column and this formula {People#Emails} to achieve this.

Does this work for what you need? Let me know

posts.author: DaliborVasiljevic (Dalibor)
posts.cookedHtml: Oh, this is awesome! Haven’t got a clue Formula is so powerful. I will explore more. Thanks! Case closed

posts.author: malia.monday (Malia)
posts.cookedHtml: Absolutely, happy to help! Feel free to follow-up if any other questions come up!

posts.author: ECH (Emmanuel CHERIGIE)
posts.cookedHtml: Hi Malia! Does the opposite exist? I mean, from the email address, assign an item to a people.

posts.author: alex_m_89 (Alex Miller)
posts.cookedHtml: @ECH not natively unfortunately. theres no built in automation that matches an email string to a user and assigns them. what i do instead is use a make (integromat) scenario that watches for changes in the email column, looks up the user by email via the monday api, then assigns them. bit of setup but works reliably once its running.
